finding_results.evidence: All Dan-interiority intrusions in Mira's POV section (post section break) have been eliminated. (1) chapters/chapter_11.md:188 — 'Dan had seen this gesture before' replaced with 'It was the same gesture she'd made,' shifting to Mira's self-awareness. (2) chapters/chapter_11.md:196-198 — 'Dan thought about the dog in the lamp' replaced with Dan mentioning the story in dialogue and Mira recalling it independently from her own memory. (3) chapters/chapter_11.md:214 — 'the most unguarded thing Dan had heard from her in weeks' replaced with 'rawer than she'd intended,' shifting to Mira's self-awareness. (4) chapters/chapter_11.md:220-222 — Dan's internal processing of next quarter's report replaced with 'Mira finished the sentence in her head,' clearly establishing Mira's interiority. (5) chapters/chapter_11.md:236 — 'because it was the only thing he could think of' (Dan's reasoning) replaced with 'Not the way he usually said things — qualifying, softening, leaving himself a way out,' rendering Dan's speech through Mira's external observation.
finding_results.notes: Every sentence of interiority after line 160 is now attributable to Mira. Dan's internal states are rendered either through his dialogue, through Mira's observation of his physical behavior and voice, or through Mira's own knowledge of what Dan is likely thinking (filtered through her POV). No 'Dan thought,' 'Dan understood,' or 'Dan could hear [internal processing]' constructions remain in the Mira section.

finding_results.evidence: Chapter text at chapters/chapter_11.md:126 ('Theo had been drawing their building'), chapters/chapter_11.md:190 ('This building. Where we live'), and chapters/chapter_11.md:200 ('He's been drawing our apartment') consistently identifies the Kessler apartment building as Theo's drawing subject. This aligns with the chapter spine ('drawing their apartment building'), the objects entry ('apartment-building drawings'), and Theo's state_transitions entry ('drawing the apartment building'). The environmental_constants entry ('the building across the street') is the sole outlier in a continuity sheet that is internally inconsistent on this point.
finding_results.notes: Per the revision packet's strong recommendation (option a), the chapter text is preserved as-is. The emotional architecture of the chapter depends on Theo drawing the building he lives in (an act of holding something he fears losing), not the building across the street (which would be mere observation). The environmental_constants entry in continuity_sheet.json should be updated to match the chapter text, spine, objects, and state_transitions entries. This update is outside the scope of chapter revision.

finding_results.evidence: The 'machine measures but cannot know' formulation now appears exactly twice: (1) chapters/chapter_11.md:156 — Dan's interiority: 'a boy the machine could measure but would never sit with on the floor while he explained why the dog in the lamp didn't have a name.' (2) chapters/chapter_11.md:228 — Mira's dialogue: 'It can tell you he draws the same building forty-one times but it can't tell you why. It can tell you his resilience is at the sixty-fourth percentile but it doesn't know he laughs at his own knock-knock jokes...' The near-duplicate at former line 216 has been replaced with a fresh formulation in Mira's interiority (chapters/chapter_11.md:222): 'as though drawing it enough times from enough angles could keep the inside from changing.' The final paragraphs (chapters/chapter_11.md:234-240) contain zero narrator-voice thesis declarations — the insight is enacted through specific details (knock-knock jokes, one-sock habit, drawings) and the closing sensory image (pencil, refrigerator, Mira's knee) without the narrator explaining what they mean.
finding_results.notes: The dog-in-the-lamp image now appears at three points with distinct angles: (1) line 82 — Dan's extended memory establishing the story for the reader; (2) line 156 — Dan's thesis statement connecting it to the machine; (3) line 196 — Dan names it in dialogue, Mira recalls finding it independently (revealing both parents read the story without telling each other). None of these reuse the 'quantify/measure but cannot sit with' construction except the single retained instance at line 156.

finding_results.evidence: Three new darkly comic moments added where institutional language provokes specifically comic POV-character reactions: (1) chapters/chapter_11.md:74 — Dan recognizes 'performance-anxiety profile' from his own corporate HR review ('He'd been thirty-eight and a vice president. Theo was nine. The machine had diagnosed them with the same condition, and it hadn't bothered with the euphemism.'). (2) chapters/chapter_11.md:106 — bathroom-visit frequency foregrounded as lead detail, with Dan noting the machine 'produced a full paragraph of analysis about a nine-year-old's bladder with more methodological rigor than anything Dan had generated in his last year of actual employment,' plus the parenthetical translation of 'selective eating patterns with consistent rejection of unfamiliar protein sources' as 'meant his kid was a picky eater.' (3) chapters/chapter_11.md:152 — Dan recognizes 'Adequate' as the dead-center corporate performance rating ('The machine had scored Theo's ability to endure his parents' marriage on the same scale Dan's old company used for evaluating slide decks.'). These coexist with the chapter's emotional devastation — the comedy sharpens the horror rather than diluting it.
finding_results.notes: The comic register emerges from Dan's specific relationship to corporate vocabulary (his stress-tell per the style bible: corporate language mixed with grounded physical observation). The humor lives in the gap between institutional classification and parental knowledge — the same gap the chapter explores thematically. Existing comic moments (line 37 dandelion logo, line 66 fractions joke) are preserved. Total comic beats: 5+.

finding_results.evidence: Revised 2 of 3 instances. chapters/chapter_11.md:158 — replaced 'the way people looked at screens when they'd stopped reading' with Dan-specific observation: 'Her eyes had stopped moving across the lines. She was looking through the screen at whatever the words had built behind it.' chapters/chapter_11.md:210 — replaced 'She said the phrase the way she'd say the name of something diagnosed in a person she loved' with direct rendering: 'She handled the phrase like a diagnosis — carefully, as if pronouncing it right could make it manageable.' The revised instances now use character-specific description rather than the universalizing formula.

finding_results.evidence: Reduced from 6 to 4 instances with varied constructions. Added: chapters/chapter_11.md:212 (after bathroom trip: 'the scratch of pencil on paper resumed behind it' — arrives after 54 lines of silence from the motif, carrying fresh weight). Also revised chapters/chapter_11.md:166 for sensory consistency: changed 'The pencil-tapping' to 'The pencil in Theo's room had gone quiet' to resolve tap/scratch inconsistency and create a more evocative silence beat.
finding_results.notes: 4 pencil-sound instances remain. 'The pencil scratched' exact construction appears once (line 158), under the ≤2 limit. The four constructions are now distinct: 'tap' (29), 'scratched' (158), 'scratch...resumed' (212), 'pencil on paper' (240). The motif's final instances arrive after Theo's bathroom trip, where his physical movement through the apartment refreshes the reader's awareness of the sound.

pass_summaries.summary: All four findings addressed. CH11-CRAFT-01 (HIGH): eliminated five instances of head-hopping into Dan's interiority within Mira's POV section by converting to dialogue, Mira's own memories, or Mira's external observation of Dan. CH11-CRAFT-02 (HIGH): chapter text preserved identifying Theo's drawing subject as the Kessler apartment building, consistent with spine/objects/state_transitions; the environmental_constants outlier in the continuity sheet requires separate update. CH11-CRAFT-03 (MEDIUM): reduced thesis formulations from five to two (Dan's interiority at line 156, Mira's dialogue at line 228); eliminated near-duplicate at former line 216 with fresh Mira-POV formulation; compressed final paragraphs to trust specific details over narrator declaration. CH11-AWARD-01 (MEDIUM): added three darkly comic moments exploiting the gap between corporate-clinical language and parental reality, coexisting with the chapter's emotional force. Heading contract preserved. Dialogue contractions verified against style bible profiles. No new continuity contradictions introduced. No sanitization.

All four findings resolved. CH11-PROSE-01: negation-correction pattern reduced from 8 to 4 identified instances (plus 1 unflagged instance also revised). CH11-PROSE-02: universalizing construction reduced from 3 to 1 instance, with replacements using character-specific observation. CH11-PROSE-03: 'particular' reduced from 5 narrator-prose instances to 1, keeping the most evocative usage. CH11-PROSE-04: pencil motif reduced from 6 to 4 instances with varied constructions and improved spacing; the motif's resumed appearance after Theo's bathroom trip carries fresh weight. Additional copyedit work: fixed sensory inconsistency (tap/scratch) at line 166, revised Mira's line at 200 to avoid verbatim echo of style-bible example line, fixed elliptical clipping in line 214 ('more than strategy' revised to 'something past anger, past strategy'). No continuity contradictions introduced; all ages, dates, object states, and spatial details verified against continuity sheet. Heading contract preserved. No sanitization: the chapter's emotional rawness (the machine's clinical language applied to a child, the parents' shame, the 'fucking motif persistence' profanity) is maintained at full specificity.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=8, PARTIAL=0, UNRESOLVED=0).
